content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR
POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
May 15,
1996
Mr.
Eduardo A.
Rivera
Attorney at Law
430 Mary Avenue
Post Office Box 1587
Calexico, California
92231
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A—96—139
Dear Mr.
Rivera:
This is in response to your request for advice regarding the
conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the
“Act”) •1
QUESTION
As a Calexico City Councilman,
may you participate in matters
which the Hefferxnan Memorial Hospital District brings before the
council concerning the bankruptcy reorganization and the joint
powers agreement,
or does your previous legal work for the
hbspital district create a conflict of interest?
CONCLUSION
The hospital district has not been a source of income to you
in the past twelve months,
but the district owes you payment for
legal services.
Once you have effectively renounced any claim you
may have against the hospital district for payment of legal
services,
you may participat in council decisions regarding the
hospital district.
FACTS
You are a city councilmeinber for the City of Calexico and an
attorney in private practice operating as
a sole—practitioner.
Prior to your election to the Calexico City Council,
you served as
counsel for approximately one year to the local hospital district
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
Commission regulations
appear at Title 2,
Sections 18000-18995 of the California Code of
Regulations.
428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660


File No.
A—96—139
Page2
which is a public entity.
You resigned your position as counsel
to the hospital district in January of 1996.
Because of financial difficulties,
the hospital district
never paid your office any attorneys fees,
per diems,
expense
money or,
in fact,
any monies at all for services rendered during
your legal representation of them.
The hospital district filed
for bankruptcy under Chapter
9 of the Bankruptcy Code and employed
counsel for the proceedings.
You have not filed nor do you intend
to file a claim in the bankruptcy proceedings for the services
rendered by your office to the hospital district.
The claims bar
date,
as set by the bankruptcy trustee,
is June 7,
1996,
and you
will file a letter with the bankruptcy attorneys indicating your
decision not to file a claim in the proceedings.
Within the next 90 days,
the hospital district,
as part of
its reorganization plan under the Chapter
9 proceedings, will ask
the Calexico City Council to participate in a limited purpose
joint powers agreement between the hospital district and the City
of Calexico which may lead to the reopening of the hospital in the
City of Calexico.
ANALYSIS
Section 87100 prohibits public officials from making,
participating in,
or using their official position to influence a
governmental decision in which they know or have reason to know
they have a financial interest.
An official has a financial
interest in a governmental decision within the meaning of Section
87100 if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have
a material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on
the public generally,
on the official,
or on a member of the
official’s immediate family or on:
(a)
Any business entity in which the public
official has a direct or indirect investment worth
one thousand dollars
($1,000)
or more.
(b)
Any real property in which the public
official has a direct or indirect interest worth
one thousand dollars
($1,000)
or more.
(c)
Any source of income,
other than gifts
and other than loans by a commercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to
official status,
aggregating two hundred fifty
dollars
($250)
or more in value provided to,
received by or promised, to the public official
within
12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made.


File No.
A—96—139
Page
3
(d)
Any business entity in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee,
or holds any position of management.
(e)
Any donor of,
or any intermediary or
agent for a donor of,
a gift or gifts aggregating
[two hundred eighty dollars
($280)]
or more in
value provided to,
received by,
or promised to the
public official within
12 months prior to the time
when the decision is made.
For purposes of this section,
indirect
investment or interest means any investment or
interest owned by the spouse or dependent child of
a public official,
by an agent on behalf of a
public official,
or by a business entity or trust
in which the official,
the official’s agents,
spouse,
and dependent children own directly,
indirectly,
or beneficially a 10-percent interest
or greater.
(Section 87103.)
You performed legal services for the hospital district.
Because of its financial difficulties,
however,
the hospital
district never paid your office any monies for such services.
You
did not receive any attorney fees, per diem,
expense money,
or any
other monies from the hospital district during the time you
represented the district.
In addition,
you have not filed and do not intend to file a
claim against the hospital district in the Chapter
9 bankruptcy
proceedings requesting payment for legal services.
You plan to
file a letter with the bankruptcy attorneys stating your intention
not to file a claim in the Chapter
9 proceedings.
Once you have effectively renounced any and all claims you
may have against the hospital district, the hospital district will
not be considered a source of income to you under Section
87103 (c).
The hospital district has not been a source of income
to you in the past
12 months.
In addition,
you will not have any
expectation of future income or “promised income” from the
hospital district once your renounce your claim.
(See Waggoner
Advice Letter,
No. A-93-3ll and Libow Advice Letter,
No.
1-91-461,
pp.
13-14.)
Therefore,
you will not have an economic interest in
the hospital district under Section 87103(c)
once you have
renounced your claim.
Assuming the hospital district is not an economic interest of
yours based on any other subdivision of Section 87103,
you may
participate in decisions of the Calexico City Council regarding
the hospital district bankruptcy reorganization and joint powers
agreement.


File No.
A—96—139
Page
4
I trust this answers your question.
If you have any further
questions regarding this matter, please contact me at 916/322-
5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
Hyla .
wagner
Counsel,
Legal Division
SGC:HPW: ak
